info.title: Publication Register (Magic Mapper)
info.description: Magic Mapper version - Uses dedicated SQL tables for high-performance schema storage. The publication register is the core register of the OpenCatalogi application. It manages all content types needed for creating and maintaining a catalog website.

x-openregister.description: Magic Mapper version - Uses dedicated SQL tables for high-performance schema storage

publication.title: Publication
publication.description: The publication register is the core register of the OpenCatalogi application. It manages all content types needed for creating and maintaining a catalog website, including catalogs, listings, organizations, publications, themes, pages, menus, and glossary items. This register enables the creation of rich, navigable catalog websites with proper content organization and user-friendly navigation.

publication.comment: High-volume content schema - main content type with frequent queries for search, filtering, and display

catalog.comment: Frequently queried for navigation and organization - benefits from SQL indexing and joins

listing.comment: High query volume for search, filtering, and synchronization operations

page.comment: Frequently accessed for website navigation - complex content structure with nested blocks

menu.comment: Frequently queried for navigation structure - hierarchical data benefits from SQL

organization.comment: Frequently queried for filtering and authentication - benefits from SQL indexing

theme.comment: Category schema for organizing publications - benefits from SQL indexing for filtering

glossary.comment: Definitions and terms - benefits from SQL indexing for search and lookups

usageCounter.comment: Internal aggregate usage counters (publication, date, kind, count) - high write volume, dedicated table; never published, excluded from public surfaces (ANA-002)

publication.title: Publication
publication.summary: A single publication entry within the catalog
publication.description: A publication represents an individual piece of content within the catalog. It can be associated with themes and schemas, and is the primary unit of content that users can view and interact with. Publications can be linked to specific organizations and can be either public or private.

organization.description: Reference to the organization that owns this publication
organization.title: Organization

themes.description: List of themes associated with this publication
themes.title: Themes

publicatiedatum.description: Publication date (WOO/DIWOO) — when set to a date in the past, this publication is live and publicly accessible
publicatiedatum.title: Publication Date

depublicatiedatum.description: Depublication date (WOO/DIWOO) — when set and in the past, this publication is withdrawn from public access. A future value schedules automatic depublication via the OpenRegister published-predicate.
depublicatiedatum.title: Depublication Date

retentionCategory.description: Selectielijst / WOO information-category reference that keys the statutory retention term (RET-003).
retentionCategory.title: Retention Category

retentionTermMonths.description: Statutory retention term in months, counted from the publication date (RET-003).
retentionTermMonths.title: Retention Term (Months)

retentionAction.description: Action taken when the retention term expires: review (flag only), depublish (set depublicatiedatum), or archive (lifecycle transition) (RET-003).
retentionAction.title: Retention Action

retentionExpiresAt.description: Computed retention expiry = publication date + retentionTermMonths. May be manually overridden, in which case retentionNote is required (RET-003).
retentionExpiresAt.title: Retention Expires At

retentionNote.description: Legal basis or exception rationale for the retention decision; mandatory when retentionExpiresAt is manually overridden or extended (RET-003).
retentionNote.title: Retention Note

retentionLastEvaluatedAt.description: Idempotency marker recording the date the retention evaluation job last actioned this publication (RET-005).
retentionLastEvaluatedAt.title: Retention Last Evaluated At

status.description: Lifecycle status of the publication; the archived state retains the object internally while removing it from every public surface (RET-006).
status.title: Status

archive.description: Archive a publication on retention expiry — removes it from public surfaces while retaining it internally with full history (RET-006).

countByStatus.description: Publication count grouped by lifecycle status (published/archived) for RET-006 retention reporting (LST-003/DSH-010).

document.title: Document
document.summary: A document attached to a publication, discoverable via the public full-text search endpoint
document.description: A document represents a file (e.g. PDF, DOCX) attached to a publication. Documents are schema-discoverable so the public full-text search endpoint (SCH-PFTS-005) can surface them alongside publications, discriminated by @self.schema.

filename.description: The original filename of the document
filename.title: Filename

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the document
mimeType.title: MIME Type

publication.description: Summary of the publication this document is attached to
publication.title: Publication

id.description: The UUID of the linked publication
id.title: Publication ID

slug.description: The slug of the linked publication
slug.title: Publication Slug

title.description: The title of the linked publication
title.title: Publication Title

organization.description: Reference to the organization that owns this document
organization.title: Organization

publicatiedatum.description: Publication date (WOO/DIWOO) — when set to a date in the past, this document is live and publicly accessible
publicatiedatum.title: Publication Date

depublicatiedatum.description: Depublication date (WOO/DIWOO) — when set and in the past, this document is withdrawn from public access
depublicatiedatum.title: Depublication Date

promoteToBeta.description: Promote a catalog from development to beta.

promoteToStable.description: Promote a beta catalog to stable.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.summary: A collection of publications organized by theme and schema
catalog.description: A catalog represents a curated collection of publications that can be organized by themes and schemas. It serves as the main organizational unit for content, allowing administrators to group related publications together and make them accessible through a unified interface. Catalogs can be public or private and can be associated with specific organizations.

listed.description: Whether the catalog is publicly listed
listed.title: Listed

organization.description: Reference to the organization that owns this catalog
organization.title: Organization

registers.description: List of register IDs associated with this catalog
registers.title: Registers

schemas.description: List of schema IDs associated with this catalog
schemas.title: Schemas

status.description: The Lifecycle status of the catalog
status.title: Status

view.description: Reference to the view or filter set ID in OpenRegister that defines additional filtering for this catalog
view.title: View

slug.description: URL-friendly identifier for the catalog, used in publication endpoints (e.g., 'publications', 'datasets')
slug.title: Slug

hasWooSitemap.description: If this catalog needs Woo specific sitemap or not, meant for Woo catalgos
hasWooSitemap.title: Has WOO Sitemap

published.description: Publication date — when set to a date in the past, this catalog becomes publicly accessible
published.title: Published Date

promoteToBeta.description: Promote a listing from development to beta.

promoteToStable.description: Promote a beta listing to stable.

listing.title: Listing
listing.summary: A filtered view of publications within a catalog
listing.description: A listing provides a customizable view of publications within a catalog. It allows for the creation of specific publication collections based on filters, sorting, and presentation preferences. Listings can be used to create featured sections, topic-based collections, or any other organized view of publications.

catalogusId.description: ID of the catalog this listing belongs to
catalogusId.title: Catalog ID

publications.description: Publications API endpoint URL for the listing
publications.title: Publications URL

statusMessage.description: Message of the latest status test
statusMessage.title: Status Message

statusCode.description: HTTP status code of the listing
statusCode.title: Status Code

integrationLevel.description: The level of integration the listing is used in
integrationLevel.title: Integration Level

default.description: Whether this is the default listing
default.title: Default

schemas.description: Types of publications in this listing
schemas.title: Schemas

version.description: The version of OpenCatalogi the source of the listing runs
version.title: Version

published.description: Publication date — when set to a date in the past, this listing becomes publicly accessible
published.title: Published Date

organization.title: Organization
organization.summary: An organization that publishes or manages catalogs
organization.description: Organizations represent entities that publish or manage catalogs. They can be government agencies, companies, or other institutions. Each organization can have multiple catalogs and is identified by various standardized identifiers like OIN, RSIN, and PKI certificates.

tooiIdentifier.description: Full TOOI organisatie identifier URI (https://identifier.overheid.nl/tooi/id/...) used as the diwoo:publisher @resource in DIWOO Woo-index feeds
tooiIdentifier.title: TOOI Identifier URI

image.description: URL to the organization's logo or image
image.title: Image

page.title: Page
page.summary: A content page for static information
page.description: Pages are used to create static content within the catalog website. They can contain information about how to use the catalog, contact information, about pages, or any other static content that needs to be accessible through the website. Pages are accessible through their unique slugs and can be organized in the navigation menu.

slug.description: URL-friendly version of the page name
slug.title: Slug

groups.description: Nextcloud groups that have access to this content block
groups.title: Access Groups

hideAfterLogin.description: Whether to hide this content block after user login
hideAfterLogin.title: Hide After Login

hideBeforeLogin.description: Whether to hide this content block before user login
hideBeforeLogin.title: Hide Before Login

groups.description: Nextcloud groups that have access to this page
groups.title: Groups

hideAfterLogin.description: Whether to hide this page after user login
hideAfterLogin.title: Hide After Login

hideBeforeLogin.description: Whether to hide this page before user login
hideBeforeLogin.title: Hide Before Login

theme.title: Theme
theme.description: Themes are used to categorize publications within catalogs. They provide a way to group related publications together, making it easier for users to find content they're interested in. Themes can be hierarchical and can be used for navigation and filtering purposes.

link.description: Button/link text for the theme card
link.title: Link Text

url.description: Destination URL when clicking the theme card link
url.title: URL

icon.description: Icon identifier for the theme card
icon.title: Icon

isExternal.description: Whether the link opens in a new tab
isExternal.title: Is External

menu.title: Menu
menu.summary: Navigation structure for the catalog website
menu.description: Menus define the navigation structure of the catalog website. They allow administrators to organize pages, listings, and other content into a hierarchical navigation system. Multiple menus can be created for different purposes, such as main navigation, footer links, or specialized content groupings.

order.description: Order/position of the menu item within the menu
order.title: Order

link.description: URL or path for the menu item
link.title: Link

groups.description: Nextcloud groups that have access to this menu item
groups.title: Access Groups

hideAfterLogin.description: Whether to hide this menu item after user login
hideAfterLogin.title: Hide After Login

hideBeforeLogin.description: Whether to hide this menu item before user login
hideBeforeLogin.title: Hide Before Login

order.description: Order/position of the submenu item within its parent
order.title: Order

link.description: URL or path for the submenu item
link.title: Link

groups.description: Nextcloud groups that have access to this submenu item
groups.title: Access Groups

hideAfterLogin.description: Whether to hide this submenu item after user login
hideAfterLogin.title: Hide After Login

hideBeforeLogin.description: Whether to hide this submenu item before user login
hideBeforeLogin.title: Hide Before Login

groups.description: Nextcloud groups that have access to this menu
groups.title: Groups

hideBeforeLogin.description: Whether to hide this menu before user login
hideBeforeLogin.title: Hide Before Login

icon.description: Pick an icon (Material Design) or paste custom SVG.
icon.title: Icon

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.summary: Definitions and explanations of terms used in the catalog
glossary.description: The glossary provides definitions and explanations for terms used throughout the catalog. It helps users understand specialized terminology, acronyms, and concepts. Each glossary entry can include detailed explanations, external references, and related keywords to help users find and understand relevant information.

externalLink.description: URL to external reference or documentation
externalLink.title: External Link

usageCounter.title: UsageCounter
usageCounter.description: Internal plumbing schema holding privacy-safe daily aggregate usage counters for publications: one object per (publication, date, kind). It stores ONLY a publication reference, a day, a kind (view/download), an integer count, and an optional catalog slug for roll-ups — no IP address, user agent, session id, referrer, or any request-derived attribute is ever stored (ANA-002). It is never published, is excluded from catalogs, sitemaps, DCAT, search, and federation, and is RBAC-restricted to the app and officer roles.

catalog.description: Catalog slug for roll-up labelling (not personal data)
catalog.title: Catalog

count.description: Non-negative aggregate count for this (publication, date, kind)
count.title: Count

objects.title: Publications
objects.description: This catalog contains all public publications available in OpenCatalogi.

data.content: This is the home page of your OpenCatalogi installation. Browse our publications catalog to find what you're looking for.
OpenCatalogi is an open source publication catalog system built on Nextcloud.

objects.name: Default Organization
objects.summary: The default organization for this OpenCatalogi instance

objects.title: Voorbeeld: Jaarverslag 2024 — Gemeente Voorbeeld
objects.summary: Voorbeeld-publicatie (seed data) — fictief jaarverslag van een fictieve gemeente. Aanwezig na fresh install om de search-functionaliteit meteen bruikbaar te maken.
objects.description: **Voorbeeld / seed data** — dit is geen echte publicatie. Bij een fresh install van OpenCatalogi wordt deze rij automatisch geladen zodat het publieke search-endpoint (`/apps/opencatalogi/api/search`) meteen iets terug kan geven. Vervang of verwijder deze rij zodra de echte publicatie-content geïmporteerd is. Gemeente Voorbeeld is een fictieve gemeente en representeert geen echte overheidsorganisatie.

objects.title: Voorbeeld: Conduction Kwartaalrapport Q1 2026
objects.summary: Voorbeeld-publicatie (seed data) — kwartaalrapport van Conduction B.V., de maintainer van OpenCatalogi. Aanwezig na fresh install om de search-functionaliteit meteen bruikbaar te maken.
objects.description: **Voorbeeld / seed data** — dit is geen echte publicatie. Bij een fresh install van OpenCatalogi wordt deze rij automatisch geladen zodat het publieke search-endpoint (`/apps/opencatalogi/api/search`) meteen iets terug kan geven. Vervang of verwijder deze rij zodra de echte publicatie-content geïmporteerd is.

objects.title: Voorbeeld: Jaarverslag 2024 (PDF)
objects.summary: Voorbeeld-document (seed data) — fictieve PDF-bijlage bij het voorbeeld-jaarverslag. Aanwezig na fresh install.
objects.description: **Voorbeeld / seed data** — dit is geen echte PDF. Bij een fresh install wordt deze rij automatisch geladen om de search-response mixed-typed te maken. Vervang of verwijder deze rij zodra echte documenten geïmporteerd zijn.

objects.description: **Voorbeeld / seed data** — dit is geen echte PDF. Bij een fresh install wordt deze rij automatisch geladen om de search-response mixed-typed te maken. Vervang of verwijder deze rij zodra echte documenten geïmporteerd zijn.
